channel.description: My personal corner of the internet.

item.title: Building this website
item.dc:creator: Sebastian Montero
item.description:
This is going to be a bit of a meta post about how I built this website.

My previous website was a one page static website that closely resambles the about section of this current website. At the time I was learnign React and I wanted to to practice building a dynamic website that eventually could be a kind of website/web app hybrid. I initally hosted this in Firebase thinkign that I could one day use other Firebase features to implement into this mystical and abstract web app I had in my mind. Of course this did not happen.

Over time I just kept the website as it was until a few months ago, when I wanted to update the data in it. I had to recreate the repository, download the React and Firebase toolkits, authenticate, etc. just to update the text of my, now outdated, site website. Something had to change, I just didn't know where to start.

Last week I came across Nikola and decided to spend the whole weekend installing, tweaking and setting this current site up. I quickly realised that a few hours of work go a long way - Nikola provides a great infrastructure to write blogs, create pages (that I used to share my music!), and a very easy deployment path relying on GitHub pages. The hours I spent setting this up are totally worth it.

This type of work reminds me of black triangles, large infrastructure efforts that enable the creation of tools (and in this case content) for the future.

Thanks god I am not using my previous React monstrosity, now I am dealing with HTML templates and CSS - which even though doesn't make things better, it provides a great building ground for the things I wanted to do in the first place: a fast and easy way to write and share with the internet.

item.title: First!
item.dc:creator: Sebastian Montero
item.description: This is my first entry to my new blog. I decided to create a blog to start writing more often about anything that I find interesting. This could be books, movies, papers, art, music etc etc etc. My idea is that this will be a personal outlet to transmit information and at the same time a way for me to preserve thoughts and ideas.
